title: bom
text: bom Connector option that determines whether a Byte Order Mark (BOM) is required in Unicode files accessed by the TXT connector. The behavior for a specific entity can be specified using the /bom switch in an entity-to-connector assignment. [DRIVER_SETTINGS USYS$TXT_PARAMS bom = yes | no | require [ENTITIES] Entity $TXT: FileName /bom = yes | no | require Values Value Meaning yes The connector writes a BOM but allows Unicode files with or without the BOM. no The connector does not write a BOM but allows Unicode files with or without the BOM. require | req The connector writes a BOM and requires Unicode files to contain a BOM. This is the default. Description The bom connector option only has an effect for Unicode files. By default, the TXT connector writes and expects a Byte Order Mark (BOM) in any Unicode file. This is when one or more of the following conditions are met: The uni connector option is used. The $DEF_CHARSET is UTF8 . The /uni switch is used on a particular file. The absence of a BOM results in an error, which can be a problem in some circumstances, such as when: The TXT 2.0 connector is used to access files created with the TXT 1.0 connector. Third-party applications that do not recognize or provide a BOM may also read and write the files. In this case, you can use the bom connector option to make BOM handling more flexible. /bom The BOM handling for a specific entity can be specified using the /bom switch in an entity-to-connector assignment. If the /bom switch is used, it overrides the value specified by the bom connector option in USYS$TXT_PARAMS . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
